title: fix build with varlink

comments.body:
We really need to get a build test with the varlink tag going so we don't regress again here.

This LGTM though. Do we need a 2.1.1? I kind of hope not...
LGTM

> We really need to get a build test with the varlink tag going so we don't regress again here.
> 
> This LGTM though. Do we need a 2.1.1? I kind of hope not...

Na I can keep this patch in dist-git as well. Also, I'll check on a CI or Makefile target to build with varlink.
ping @cevich , in the cirrus config file, I do see a `varlink_api_task` which has `BUILDTAGS=varlink` blah blah. Does this not get run during CI ?
LGTM
@lsm5 it's there, it ran and passed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7734/checks?check_run_id=1151229825

@cevich so `/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" varlink_api_generate |& ${TIMESTAMP}` actually runs `make BUILDTAGS="varlink"`, yes? But `make BUILDTAGS="varlink"` fails on the current master branch

> @lsm5 it's there, it ran and passed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7734/checks?check_run_id=1151229825

Umm, that one should pass as it fixes the build issue.

I suspect the one in question would be 
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4535093320482816

> @cevich ...actually runs make BUILDTAGS="varlink", yes? 

The task called "varlink_api" effectively runs `make BUILDTAGS=varlink varlink_api_generate`.  I'm not sure what that does under the covers of the `Makefile`.

It sounds like maybe you're looking for a new task which runs `make BUILDTAGS="varlink" podman` (or `podman-remote`)?

@lsm5 maybe could this be a quick-fix:

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index a11bbbe61..196b4cf1c 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -230,6 +230,11 @@ varlink_api_task:
         - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" varlink_api_generate |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         - 'cd ${GOSRC} && ./hack/tree_status.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'

+    # Ensure that building podman with varlink completes successfully
+    build_script:
+        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" podman |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
+        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" podman-remote |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
+

 build_each_commit_task:

```

> @lsm5 maybe could this be a quick-fix:
> 
> ```diff
> diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
> index a11bbbe61..196b4cf1c 100644
> --- a/.cirrus.yml
> +++ b/.cirrus.yml
> @@ -230,6 +230,11 @@ varlink_api_task:
>          - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" varlink_api_generate |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
>          - 'cd ${GOSRC} && ./hack/tree_status.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
> 
> +    # Ensure that building podman with varlink completes successfully
> +    build_script:
> +        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" podman |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
> +        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" podman-remote |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
> +
> 
>  build_each_commit_task:
> ```

hmm, I guess `usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh BUILDTAGS="varlink" varlink_api_generate binaries |& ${TIMESTAMP}` would work too, right? Or is it preferable to separate them out?
ah I guess it's best left in a separate task. I'll update this PR.
LGTM

/lgtm
/hold
/approve

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)

@lsm5 Can you backport this against the v2.1 branch when it merges?
@mheon sure thing
CI is all green, so I backported it anyway: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7737
/hold cancel
